name: Charles Finney
title: Evangelist & Revivalist
bio: American Presbyterian minister, revivalist leader, and president of Oberlin College. He was a central figure in the Second Great Awakening and is estimated to have led over 500,000 people to Christ.
faith: Finney's dramatic conversion experience in 1821 transformed him from a lawyer to a fiery evangelist. His revival meetings featured new methods including the 'anxious bench' and extended meetings. He was also a strong advocate for abolition and women's education.

sources.title: Charles Grandison Finney — Wikipedia
sources.note: Biography of the Second Great Awakening revivalist

sources.title: The Autobiography of Charles G. Finney
sources.note: Finney's own account of his conversion and revival ministry

tagline: The father of modern revivalism who transformed American Christianity
